items.title: Daily Driver: High Frame Rates are Best Frame Rates
items.summary: Over the course of development I’d been unhappy with the game running at 30fps, as it did not feel responsive enough. I’m a big believer that if a gamer features fast action gameplay and requires quick reactions then higher frame rates and lower response time are what is needed. Over the lifetime of the project I had beeb experimenting with running the game…

items.title: Some thoughts on touch screen user interface design
items.summary: Past weeks I have been working on a new app involving personal health. The main challenge is to come up with an intuitive, natural interface that works well on small touch screen devices. But what is it that makes an touch screen interface any good? Read along for some best practices.

items.title: Tudor Black Bay 36 long-term review
items.summary: For the past six months I have been wearing the same watch, pretty much every day and night. It's a Tudor Black Bay 36 watch in steel, with a black dial. I like minimal (yet functional) design that lasts, this watch certainly ticks the right boxes. Read along to find out how wearable a smaller watch is in daily practice.

items.title: Linking Lemmid Store with kitchens
items.summary: As part of the food ordering app I am building, I needed to design a reliable way to link the app to external systems. These external systems are beyond my direct control and include different checkout registers, kitchen management systems and ticket printers. Read along for more on designing for the unknown and unreliable.

items.title: Java or Kotlin, What do you use?
items.summary: Kotlin is something which was introduced as an official language for Android development in Google I/O 2017. Since then it has grown and evolved a lot. It has multiple benefits and on top of everything, it’s really easy to use.

items.title: Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) Concepts
items.summary: Object-oriented programming (OOP) concepts are something which are fundamentals of any Object-oriented programming language. These concepts give you the power to structure your code in such a way that it’s well optimized, reusable, efficient, future proof and much more.

items.summary: This month I did some research into how health is visualised in fitness apps and games. For a new app involving personal health I am looking for an intuitive way to visualise how healthy one person is. There are many colourful approaches out there!

items.title: Scalable application design without magic
items.summary: As part of the online food ordering app I'm building, I needed to design a scalable backend infrastructure that could handle lots of concurrent users. Scalability is considered a hard problem to tackle. Often it's presented like it's something magical, done by million dollar companies using secret tools. But, there is no such thing as magic, or is there?

items.title: Designing an interface for a food ordering page
items.summary: Online ordering pages are more important than ever before. The COVID-19 virus, the resulting lockdowns and the social distancing rules have emphasised the need for a well-designed webshop user interface. This is surprisingly hard to get right!

items.title: Minimalistic road bike with Gates carbon drive
items.summary: For the past few months I have been riding a special kind of bike, the minimalistic Siegfried Road from Schindelhauer. It is unlike most road bikes as it lacks a conventional chain and derailleur. It features the Gates CDX carbon drive. It's an exercise in minimal design. After riding it for nearly 1000KM, it's time to tell you all about it.

items.title: Collections in Kotlin
items.summary: Collections are a common concept for most programming languages. It is a way of storing similar data types in a single object and iterate over it. Similar to any other language, Kotlin also has Collection defined in kotlin.collections package.

items.title: Talking tablets: what makes a great tablet?
items.summary: Earlier this year Microsoft released their brand new Surface Pro X tablet computer, in many ways this is a forward thinking device. As I like working with tablets I wondered what exactly makes a great tablet? Is Surface Pro X any good? Read along to find out.

items.title: How often does Rust change?
